content.problemStatement:
Chef has recently introduced a feature, called WA Test Cases, which allows you to see the smallest test case that your WA code has failed in. For example, you can go to any relevant problem in Practice, make a submission which gets a WA verdict, and then click on the "Debug My Code" button to see the test case.

Your job now is to find that smallest test case that a particular submission fails on. That problem has NNN test cases, and you are given the size of each of those test cases as S1,S2,…,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1​,S2​,…,SN​. You are also given a binary string VVV, which tells whether the submission has passed a particular test case or not. That is, if the ithi^{th}ith bit of VVV is 111, that means that the submission has passed the ithi^{th}ith test case. If it is 000, then it has failed that test case. Your job is the output the size of the smallest test case where the submission has failed.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer, NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers - S1,S2,…,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1​,S2​,…,SN​
The third line contains a binary string - VVV.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, a single integer, which should be the size of the smallest test case where the submission has failed.
constraints: V is a binary string of length N
At least one character of V is '0'
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains integer N, the number of test cases of the original problem. Second line contains N integers S1 S2 … SN, the sizes of those test cases. Third line contains a binary string V of length N, where V[i] = '1' means the submission passed the i‑th test case and '0' means it failed.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the size of the smallest test case on which the submission failed.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we pick the minimum size among indices where V has '0'. The computed minima are 3, 5, 5, 5 and 10 respectively.
Only the last test case fails (V[4] = '0'), its size is 8, which is the smallest failing size.
The first test case fails (size 50) while the second passes. Minimum failing size is 50.

solution.approach: For each test case iterate over the N positions. Keep a variable minFail initialized to a large value. Whenever V[i] is '0' update minFail = min(minFail, Si). After the loop output minFail. The algorithm is O(N) per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T·N) overall

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        S = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        V = next(it)
        ans = min(s for s, v in zip(S, V) if v == '0')
        out_lines.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
